          Parameter 3: uint8 lower right char column
          Parameter 4: uint8 lower right char row
          Parameter 5: uint8 color (0...15)
          Parameter 6: Boolean clear window first
          Parameter 7: Pointer to String
          (Splits String onto multiple lines if necessary.  If window is not
          large enough, user is asked to press any key to continue, which
          clears the message window and continues the message.  If DS:4A00 is
          set, prints slower than other messages to give time to read.)
09E3 0B65 Input String on bottom line of screen
          Parameter 1: Pointer to input String
          Parameter 2: Pointer to query String
          Parameter 3: uint8 query color (0...15)
          Parameter 4: uint8 maximum length of input String
          Return on stack: Pointer to input String, converted to uppercase
          (The query and input Strings combined must fit on the bottom line of
          the screen.  ASCII codes 64...96 that are not letters will be
          displayed in the GB font but returned as ASCII.  The parser also
          recognizes Backspace, Return, and Escape.)

          Parameter 1: Pointer to query String
          Parameter 2: uint8 query color (0...15)
          Return in AX: uint16
          (Maximum input length is always 6.  Input is retried until it parses
          as a valid uint16.)

          used as a file name.)

          Return in AL: uint8 char code
          (* Traps Ctrl-S, which stops any currently playing sound and changes
             the sound setting.
           * Traps Ctri-D, which with STING toggles hit point debugging.
           * Traps Ctrl-C, which with STING quits to DOS.

Segment 08AE (IDA segment 45): (uncompressed EXE - add 93C0)
Sound; SSI didn't use the primitive APIs in Crt (even the very first version of
IBM PC BASIC is easily far superior to Crt for sound).  Much of this looks like
code copied from elsewhere.
0000 006B Centiseconds elapsed since midnight
          Return in DX:AX: int32 centiseconds
          (Identical to 0709:0C3F.)

          Parameter: int16 sound effect number
            -1 silence
             0 unused
             1 unused
             2 spell other than Lightning Bolt or Fireball
             3 magic damage
             4 magic effect
             5 reduced to 0 HP
             6 ranged weapon attack launched
             7 melee attack hit
             8 lightning bolt
             9 melee attack missed
            10 moving, or CALL $BA03 when GBVM $03DE is 8
            11 fireball, or CALL $BA03 when GBVM $03DE is 10
            12 ranged weapon attack arrives at target
            13 unused
            14 unused
            15 unused

          (Only runs once.)

          Parameter: uint16 unknown
          (Runs once per sound.)

Segment 0A32 (IDA segment 47): (uncompressed EXE - add AC00 for offset)
Dos unit (TPv6PG chapter 11).  Does not have an initializer, unlike all other
units I've seen.
Begin End
000B 0044 (Lib) (Turbo Pascal Intr procedure entry)
          Parameter 1: uint8 interrupt to call
          Parameter 2: Pointer to registers
          (Loads registers from pointer and calls the interrupt.)
0045 006B (Lib, Unref) (Turbo Pascal Intr procedure exit)
          Parameter: Pointer to registers
          (Called after the above interrupt call returns.  Stores registers
          from the interrupt call.)
006C 008D (Lib, Unref, Unexec) Get system date (Turbo Pascal GetDate procedure)
          Parameter 1: Pointer to uint16 year
          Parameter 2: Pointer to uint8 month
          Parameter 3: Pointer to uint8 day
          Parameter 4: Pointer to uint8 day of week
          Sets all variables accordingly.
008E 00A1 (Lib, Unref, Unexec) Set system date (Turbo Pascal SetDate procedure)
          Parameter 1: uint16 year
          Parameter 2: uint8 month
          Parameter 3: uint8 day
00A2 00C6 (Lib) Get system time (Turbo Pascal GetTime procedure)
          Parameter 1: Pointer to uint8 hours
          Parameter 2: Pointer to uint8 minutes
          Parameter 3: Pointer to uint8 seconds
          Parameter 4: Pointer to uint8 centiseconds
          Sets all variables accordingly.
          (Each DOS clock cycle advances the time by ~6 centiseconds.)
00C7 00DD (Lib, Unref, Unexec) Set system time (Turbo Pascal SetTime procedure)
          Parameter 1: uint8 hours
          Parameter 2: uint8 minutes
          Parameter 3: uint8 seconds
          Parameter 4: uint8 centiseconds
00DE 00DF (segment byte alignment)

Segment 0A40 (IDA segment 48): (uncompressed EXE - add ACE0 for offset)
Crt unit (TPv6PG chapter 15).
Begin End

002E 0096 (Lib) CRT initialization 2

          Parameter in AL: video mode (for INT 10 AH 00)

          Parameter: uint16 video mode

          Parameter: uint16 duration to delay in milliseconds
02BD 02C8 (Lib) Empty loop
02C9 02F5 (Lib, Unref, Unexec) (Turbo Pascal Sound procedure)
          Parameter: uint16 frequency of tone in hz
          (The PC speaker plays the tone indefinitely.)
02F6 02FC (Lib, Unref, Unexec) (Turbo Pascal NoSound procedure)
02FD 030E (Lib) (Turbo Pascal KeyPressed function)
          Return in AL: Boolean if a key has been pressed
          (Does not remove any char from the keyboard buffer.  Metakeys like
          Shift by themselves do not count.)
030F 0330 (Lib) (Turbo Pascal ReadKey function)
          Return in AL: uint8 char code (0 means call again for extended code)
          (Function most likely to be currently active, as it's the one that
          awaits keyboard input.)
0331 035B (Lib) (Turbo Pascal AssignCrt procedure)
          Parameter: Pointer to console File
035C 0393 (Lib) Assign console File function pointers
          Parameter: Pointer to console File
          Return in AX: 0
          (OpenFunc for a console File.)

          Parameter: Pointer to console File
          Return in AX: 0
          (Default way of reading the keyboard, but not used here.)
0441 0471 (Lib, Unref) Output console File write
          Parameter: Pointer to console File
          Return in AX: 0
          (Default way of printing text, but not usually used here.)
0472 0476 (Lib, Unref, Unexec) Dummy console File function
          Parameter: Pointer to console File
          Return in AX: 0
0477 047D (Lib) Print CR + LF to screen (fallthrough to ...)
047E 04D6 (Lib) Print char to screen (using BIOS calls)
          Parameter in AL: uint8 char code
04D7 04F7 (Lib) Scroll screen up
04F8 04FE (Lib) Call INT 10 AH 03 (get cursor position and size)
          Return in CH: start scan line
          Return in CL: end scan line
          Return in DH: row
          Return in DL: column

          Parameter in BH: page number (usually 0)
          Parameter in DH: row
          Parameter in DL: column
0506 05A2 (Lib) Print char to screen (using direct memory writes)
          Parameter in AL: uint8 char code

          (Preserves BP, ES, DI, and SI.)
0616 061F (segment byte alignment)

Segment 0AA2 (IDA segment 49): (uncompressed EXE - add B300 for offset)
Overlay unit (TPv6PG chapter 13).  OvrInitEMS doesn't seem to be present.
Initialization is special-cased: it is not automatically called by main and
must be explicitly called by the programmer.
0000 000C (Lib) OvrReadBuf error
          (This is what gets called if OvrReadBuf returns anything not 0.)
000D 0011 (unknown)
0012 00E8 (Lib) (Turbo Pascal OvrInit procedure)
          Parameter: String with overlay filename (passed as value)
00E9 00F4 (Lib) Open overlay file with provided filename
          Parameter 1: uint8 OvrFileMode
          Parameter 2: String with overlay filename
00F5 012F (Lib) Open overlay file from executable directory
          Parameter 1: uint8 OvrFileMode
          Parameter 2: String with overlay filename
          (Called if the first open call above fails.)
0130 0187 (Lib) Open overlay file searching PATH
          Parameter 1: uint8 OvrFileMode
          Parameter 2: String with overlay filename
          (Called if the second open call above fails.)
0188 01A2 (Lib) Open overlay file
01A3 01F1 (Lib) (Turbo Pascal OvrSetBuf procedure)
          Parameter: int32 size of overlay buffer
          (Will be rounded down to the nearest 16 bytes.)
01F2 01FC (Lib) (Turbo Pascal OvrGetBuf procedure)
          Return in DX:AX: int32 size of overlay buffer
01FD 020D (Lib, Unref, Unexec) (Turbo Pascal OvrSetRetry procedure)
          Parameter: int32 size of probation area in bytes
          (Will be rounded down to the nearest 16 bytes.)
020E 0214 (Lib, Unref, Unexec) (Turbo Pascal OvrGetRetry procedure)
          Return in DX:AX: int32 size of probation area in bytes
0215 024D (Lib, Unref, Unexec) (Turbo Pascal OvrClearBuf procedure)

          Parameter in DX:AX: int32 20-bit integer
          Return in AX: uint16 quotient

          Parameter in AX: uint16
          Return in DX:AX: int32 product
0268 026B OvrReadBuf default offset
02E6 0337 (Lib, Unref) INT 3F handler
          (This may be called upon in normal program operation, unlike the
          default INT 3F handler.)
055F 055F (segment byte alignment)

Segment 0AF8 (IDA segment 50): (uncompressed EXE - add B860 for offset)
Turbo Pascal core library (System unit; TPv6PG chapter 10), which should always
be the highest non-overlay code segment.  The compiler is intelligent enough to
leave out many unused core library functions (e.g., Real square root), but not
intelligent enough to exclude all of them (e.g., RNG 8087 code).  Anything used
and documented but not present below is likely inlined (e.g., SizeOf).
Begin End
0000 00A8 (Lib) System initialization
          (Always the first thing called by every Turbo Pascal program.)
00A9 00AD (Lib, Unref, Unexec) Default HeapError function
          Return in AX: 0 (always)
00AE 00D6 (Lib, Unref) INT 24 handler
00D7 00DE (Lib, Unref) INT 3F handler (fallthrough)
          Runtime error 208
          (Default INT 3F handler; triggers if the overlay manager is not
          installed and any other overlay routine gets called.)
00DF 00E1 (Lib, Unref) INT 0 handler (fallthrough)
          Runtime error 200
          (Any untrapped division by 0 error calls here.)
00E2 00E5 (Lib) Abort program execution with runtime error (fallthrough)
          Parameter in AX: runtime error code
00E6 00E8 (Lib, Unref) INT 23 handler (fallthrough to ...)
          Runtime error 255 (undocumented error code)
00E9 01A4 (Lib) Abort execution (Turbo Pascal Halt procedure)
          Parameter in AX: uint16 exit code
01A5 01B2 (Lib) Output runtime error string to DOS console
          Parameter in BX: near pointer to C string in CS
01B3 01BE (Lib) Output runtime error code in decimal to DOS console
          Parameter in AX: runtime error code
01BF 01CC (Lib) Output digit of of runtime error code to DOS console
          Parameter in AL: runtime error code
          Parameter in CL: divisor
01CD 01D3 (Lib) Output hexadecimal value of AX to DOS console (fallthrough)
01D4 01DE (Lib) Output hexadecimal value of AL to DOS console
01DF 01E6 (Lib) Output hexadecimal digit to DOS console (fallthrough to ...)
          Parameter in AL: hexdecimal digit
01E7 01ED (Lib) Output single char to DOS console
          Parameter in AL: uint8 char code
01EE 0200 Array of interrupts to preserve.
0201 020F "Runtime error " C string
0210 0214 " at " C string
0215 0218 "." + CR + LF C string
0219 023E Borland copyright notice (technically not any kind of string)
023F 0253 (Lib) malloc equivalent (Turbo Pascal GetMem procedure)
          Parameter: uint16 size of dynamic variable to allocate
          Return in DX:AX: far pointer to allocated memory
          Runtime error 203 if memory cannot be allocated
0254 026D (Lib) free equivalent (Turbo Pascal FreeMem procedure)
          Parameter 1: Pointer to dynamic variable
          Parameter 2: uint16 size of dynamic variable
          Runtime error 204 if pointer is not valid
          (Unlike C, a Turbo Pascal programmer must track the size of
          dynamically allocated heap data.  As you might expect, this results
          in extra work and bloated code that on the other hand may better
          tolerate programmer error.)
026E 027F (Lib, Unref, Unexec) (Turbo Pascal Mark procedure)
          Parameter: Pointer to Mark
          (Not used by SSI; it's generally not a good idea to combine Mark/
          Release with GetMem/FreeMem in the same program.)
0280 029B (Lib, Unref, Unexec) (Turbo Pascal Release procedure)
          Parameter: Pointer previously assigned by Mark
029C 02B7 (Lib) (Turbo Pascal MemAvail function; fallthrough to ...)
          Return in DX:AX: int32 total free memory
02B8 0307 (Lib) (Turbo Pascal MaxAvail function)
          Return in DX:AX: int32 largest block of free memory
0308 03D2 (Lib) Find allocable block of memory
          Parameter in AX: uint16 count of bytes to allocate
          Return in DX:AX: far pointer to allocated memory
03D3 0431 (Lib) Place freed memory back in pool
04A2 04A8 (Lib) (Turbo Pascal IOResult function, with {$I-} compile flag)
          Return in AX: current value of IOResult, which is then set to 0
04A9 04B6 (Lib) Checks for fatal I/O errors, with {$I+} compile flag
          Runtime error if IOResult is non-zero
04B7 04DE (Lib, Unref, Unexec) Check range
          (With {$R+} compile flag, checks for range out-of-bounds errors.
          Pool of Radiance was globally compiled with {$R-}.)
04DF 04F6 (Lib, Unref, Unexec) Check for stack overflow
          (With {$S+} compile flag, checks for stack overflow.  Pool of
          Radiance was globally compiled with {$S-}.)
04F7 053B (Lib) (Turbo Pascal Assign procedure, text)
          Parameter 1: Pointer to File
          Parameter 2: Pointer to String with file name
053C 0566 (Lib, Unref, Unexec) (Turbo Pascal SetTextBuf procedure)
          Parameter 1: Pointer to File
          Parameter 2: Pointer to buffer
          Parameter 3: (optional) uint16 buffer size
0567 056B (Lib) (Turbo Pascal Reset procedure, text; fallthrough to ...)
          Parameter: Pointer to File
          Return in IOResult: int16 error code
056C 0570 (Lib) (Turbo Pascal Rewrite procedure, text; fallthrough to ...)
          Parameter: Pointer to File
          Return in IOResult: int16 error code
0571 05BC (Lib) (Turbo Pascal Append procedure)
          Parameter: Pointer to File
          Return in IOResult: int16 error code
05BD 05C0 (Lib, Unref, Unexec) (Turbo Pascal Flush procedure; fallthrough to
          ...)
          Parameter: Pointer to File
          Return in IOResult: int16 error code
05C1 05FC (Lib) (Turbo Pascal Close procedure, text)
          Parameter: Pointer to File
          Return in IOResult: int16 error code
05FD 060D (Lib) Call text file device driver
          Parameter at ES:DI: File
          Parameter in BX: function pointer offset in File (10 = OpenFunc,
            14 = InOutFunc, 18 = FlushFunc, 1C = CloseFunc)
          Return in IOResult: int16 error code
          (As descrived in TPv6PG pp. 251...253.)
060E 068F (Lib, Unref) (Turbo Pascal OpenFunc default)
          Parameter: Pointer to text File
          Return in AX: int16 error code
          (TPv6PG p. 252:
           * Gets file handle from DOS API.
           * Sets InOutFunc to 0AF8:063A if read-only, 0AF8:071A if write-only.
           * Sets FlushFunc to 0AF8:073F if stdout, nil otherwise.
           * Sets CloseFunc to 0AF8:075F always.)
0690 06E9 (Lib, Unexec) Move text file position to end
          Parameter at ES:DI: File
          Return in AX: int16 error code
          (Called for a text file that was opened with Append.)
06EA 0719 (Lib, Unref) (Turbo Pascal InOutFunc default, read-only text)
          Parameter at ES:DI: File
          Return in AX: int16 error code
          (TPv6PG p. 253.)
071A 073E (Lib, Unref) (Turbo Pascal InOutFunc default, write-only disk file)
          Parameter at ES:DI: File
          Return in AX: int16 error code
          (TPv6PG p. 253.)
073F 075E (Lib, Unref) (Turbo Pascal InOutFunc default, stdout)
          Parameter at ES:DI: File
          Return in AX: int16 error code
          (TPv6PG p. 253.)
075F 0777 (Lib, Unref) (Turbo Pascal CloseFunc default)
          (TPv6PG p. 253.)
0778 079B (Lib) Load registers for read from text file
          Parameter at ES:DI: File
          Return in BX: File buffer position
          Return in DX: File buffer size
          Return at ES:DI: File buffer
          Return in IOResult: 104 if File not open for input
079C 07B7 (Lib) Read char from text file
          Parameter in BX: File buffer position
          Parameter in DX: File buffer size
          Parameter at ES:DI: File buffer
          Return in AL: uint8 char code (^Z if end of file)

          Parameter at ES:DI: File

          Return in IOResult: 105 if File not open for output

          Parameter in AL: uint8 char

          Parameter in DX: File buffer size

081C 0849 (Lib) (Turbo Pascal Readln procedure)

          (The compiler put any arguments into a separate function call.)
084A 086D (Lib) Write CRLF to text file
          Parameter: Pointer to File

          (Always calls 087B.  Last call performed by Writeln; there is no
          single function that is Writeln.)

          (Like Move, and unlike C memcpy, the source is the first parameter,

          (The sign of DX:AX is not checked.)

          Return in DX:AX: shifted int32
          (May change the sign of a positive int32.)

0B0B 0B24 (Lib) strcpy equivalent (no length)
          Parameter 1: Pointer to destination String
          Parameter 2: Pointer to source String

0B25 0B48 (Lib) strncpy equivalent (with length)
          Parameter 1: Pointer to source String
          Parameter 2: Pointer to destination String
          Parameter 3: uint8 maximum count of characters to copy
          (Unlike both C strncpy and the strcpy equivalent, the source is the
          first parameter, and the destination String is not returned.)
0B49 0B56 (Lib, Unref, Unexec) strlen equivalent (Turbo Pascal Length
          function).
          Parameter: Pointer to String
          Return in AL: uint8 length of String
          (I don't know why this exists at all.  This should be, and is,
          inlined; it's very simple in Pascal so long as String length is
          not allowed to exceed 255.)

          Runtime error 200 if divisor is 0
          Runtime error 205 if floating-point overflow occurred
1196 1199 (Lib, Unref, Unexec, Wrap) Compare two Reals
          Parameter 1 in DX:BX:AX: first Real
          Parameter 2 in DI:SI:CX: second Real
          Return in FLAGS: comparison result
119A 119D (Lib, Wrap) Cast int32 to Real
          Parameter in DX:AX: int32
          Return in DX:BX:AX: Real
119E 11A5 (Lib, Wrap) Cast Real to int32 (Turbo Pascal Trunc function)
          Parameter in DX:BX:AX: Real
          Return in DX:AX: int32
          Runtime error 207 if Real is not within int32 range
          (The return is always rounded down.)
11A6 11AD (Lib, Unref, Unexec, Wrap) Cast Real to int32 (Turbo Pascal Round
          function)
          Parameter in DX:BX:AX: Real
          Return in DX:AX: int32
          Runtime error 207 if Real is not within int32 range
          (The return is rounded up if appropriate.)
11AE 11B0 Runtime error 207 (Invalid floating point operation) handler
11B1 11B9 Runtime error 205 (Floating point overflow) handler
11BA 11BF Runtime error 200 (Division by zero) handler
11C0 11D5 (Lib) Generate random integer (Turbo Pascal Random function, Word)
          Parameter: uint16 Range
          Return in AX: uint16 0 <= AX < Range (0 if Range is 0)
          (This is one of the most important functions that determines game
          behavior, so it's worth making clear that this advances the seed
          before, not after, calculating the uint16 to return.  If Range > 0,
          the top 16 bits of the seed are divided by the Range of 1...65535,
          and the remainder is returned, which is 0...65534 at the widest.  Any
          source claiming otherwise is wrong.)
11D6 11F2 (Lib, Unref, Unexec) Generate random Real 0 <= x < 1, without 80387
          coprocessor (Turbo Pascal Random function, no argument)
          Return in DX:BX:AX: pseudorandom Real
          (No digits get discarded here; the less-random lower-order seed bits
          are just less significant in the floating-point representation.
          There seems to be a slight bug in the floating-point normalization,
          but as this never is called, I can't be bothered to investigate
          further.)
11F3 120E (Lib, Unref, Unexec) Generate random Real 0 <= x < 1, with 80387
          coprocessor (Turbo Pascal Random function, no argument)
          Return in DX:BX:AX: pseudorandom Real
120F 1246 (Lib) Advance random seed
          (Turbo Pascal v4 to v7 use a linear congruential generator, with
          m=4294967296, a=134775813, c=1.  This is particularly important to
          optimize, as RNG is a fairly common performance bottleneck on older
          computers.  If you are new to reading machine code, it might be worth
          your time to figure how the code actually is multiplying by 134775813
          -- this isn't how a compiler would normally encode 32-bit integer
          multiplication.)
1247 1253 (Lib) Initialize random seed (Turbo Pascal Randomize procedure)
          (Uses the DOS system clock; there are slightly more than 65536
          possible starting values.  There is no source I know of that
          describes how this happens correctly, even though it's very simple at
          the machine code level.  This is probably because it is difficult to
          precisely express in any language higher-level than assembly.)
1254 12CD (Lib) Convert int32 to string
          Parameter in DX:AX: int32
          Return in ES:DI: string
          Return in CX: string length
12CE 1365 (Lib) Convert string to int32
          Parameter at ES:DI: string
          Return in DX:AX: int32
          Return in DI: index of problem if there is an error
1366 13B0 (Lib) (Turbo Pascal Str procedure, int32)
          Parameter 1: int32 X
          Parameter 2: int16 Width
          Parameter 3: Pointer to destination String S
          Parameter 4: uint8 maximum length of destination String
13B1 13E1 (Lib) Convert String to int32 (Turbo Pascal Val procedure, Integer)
          Parameter 1: Pointer to input String S
          Parameter 2: Pointer to int16
          Return in DX:AX: int32
          Return in int16: Code
13E2 140F (Lib) (Turbo Pascal Assign procedure, not text)
          Parameter 1: Pointer to File
          Parameter 2: Pointer to String with file name
1410 1418 (Lib) (Turbo Pascal Reset procedure, not text; fallthrough to ...)
          Parameter 1: Pointer to File
          Parameter 2: uint16 record size
          Return in IOResult: error code
1419 1474 (Lib) (Turbo Pascal Rewrite procedure, not text)
          Parameter 1: Pointer to File
          Parameter 2: uint16 record size
          Return in IOResult: error code
1475 1490 (Lib, Unref, Unexec) (Turbo Pascal Truncate procedure)
          Parameter: Pointer to File
          Return in IOResult: error code
1491 14B5 (Lib) (Turbo Pascal Close procedure, not text)
          Parameter: Pointer to File
          Return in IOResult: error code
14B6 14C4 (Lib) Check if file is open
          Parameter at ES:DI: File
          Return in IOResult: 103 if File is not open
14C5 14CB (Lib, Unref, Unexec) (Turbo Pascal Read procedure, not text;
          fallthrough to ...)
          Parameter 1: Pointer to File
          Parameter 2: Pointer to record
          Return on stack: Pointer to File
          Return in IOResult: error code
          (Only one record at a time; the compiler separates any varargs.)
14CC 14FA (Lib, Unref, Unexec) (Turbo Pascal Write procedure, not text)
          Parameter 1: Pointer to File
          Parameter 2: Pointer to record
          Return on stack: Pointer to File
          Return in IOResult: error code
          (Only one record at a time; the compiler separates any varargs.)
14FB 1501 (Lib) (Turbo Pascal BlockRead procedure; fallthrough to ...)
          Parameter 1: Pointer to File
          Parameter 2: Pointer to destination
          Parameter 3: uint16 count
          Parameter 4: Pointer to uint16 Result
          Return in IOResult: error code
1502 1562 (Lib) (Turbo Pascal BlockWrite procedure)
          Parameter 1: Pointer to File
          Parameter 2: Pointer to source
          Parameter 3: uint16 count
          Parameter 4: Pointer to uint16 Result
          Return in IOResult: error code
1563 1592 (Lib) (Turbo Pascal Seek procedure)
          Parameter 1: Pointer to File
          Parameter 2: int32 record number
1593 15A9 (Lib) (Turbo Pascal FilePos function)
          Parameter: Pointer to File
          Return in DX:AX: int32 record position
          Return in IOResult: error code
15AA 15C4 (Lib, Unref, Unexec) (Turbo Pascal FileSize function)
          Parameter: Pointer to File
          Return in DX:AX: int32 number of records
          Return in IOResult: error code
15C5 15D9 (Lib) (Turbo Pascal Eof function, not text)
          Parameter: Pointer to File
          Return in AL: Boolean
          Return in IOResult: error code
15DA 161E (Lib) seek file position information
          Parameter at ES:DI: File
          Return in DX:AX: current File position in bytes
          Return in BX:CX: size of File in bytes
          Return in IOResult: 103 if File is not open
          (Current position in file is preserved.)
161F 1635 (Lib) (Turbo Pascal Erase procedure)
          Parameter: Pointer to File
          Return in IOResult: error code
          (May not be used on an open file.)
1636 1684 (Lib, Unref, Unexec) (Turbo Pascal Rename procedure)
          Parameter 1: Pointer to File
          Parameter 2: Pointer to String with new filename
          Return in IOResult: error code
          (May not be used on an open file.)
1685 16A7 (Lib) memcpy equivalent (Turbo Pascal Move procedure)
          Parameter 1: Pointer to source
          Parameter 2: Pointer to destination
          Parameter 3: uint16 count of bytes to copy
          (Unlike C memcpy, the source is the first parameter, and there is no
          return of any pointer to the destination.)
16A8 16BB (Lib) memset equivalent (Turbo Pascal FillChar procedure)
          Parameter 1: Pointer to destination
          Parameter 2: uint16 count of bytes to fill
          Parameter 3: uint8 character to write
16BC 170A (Lib) argv equivalent (Turbo Pascal ParamStr function)
          Parameter 1: Pointer to String
          Parameter 2: uint16 index
          Return on stack: Pointer to (index)th command line parameter
170B 1711 (Lib, Unref, Unexec, Wrap) argc equivalent (Turbo Pascal ParamCount
          function)
1712 1743 (Lib) Count parameters
          Return in BX: count
1744 1794 (Lib, Unref, Unexec) (Turbo Pascal GetDir procedure)
          Parameter 1: uint8 drive (0 = current, 1 = A, 2 = B, etc.)
          Parameter 2: Pointer to String
          Parameter 3: uint16 maximum length (ignored if >79)
          Return in String: current directory on specified drive
          Return in IOResult: error code
1795 17D5 (Lib, Unref, Unexec) (Turbo Pascal ChDir procedure)
          Parameter: Pointer to String with directory name
          Return in IOResult: error code
17D6 17EA (Lib) (Turbo Pascal MkDir procedure)
          Parameter: Pointer to String with directory name
          Return in IOResult: error code
17EB 17FF (Lib, Unref, Unexec) (Turbo Pascal RmDir procedure)
          Parameter: Pointer to String with directory name
          Return in IOResult: error code
1800 181A (Lib) Convert String to C string
          (Required by some DOS interrupts.)
181B 1829 (Lib) Call INT 21
          Parameter in AH: required for all INT 21 calls
          Parameter on stack at BP-0x80: C string if call requires one
          Other possible parameters: BX, CX, DI, SI (but not DX)
          Return in IOResult: error code
182A 183C (Lib) (Turbo Pascal UpCase function)
          Parameter: uint8 Char
          Return in AL: converted Char
183D 183F (segment byte alignment; end of non-overlay machine code, DS:0000
          follows)

SECTION 12.6.3 - Overlay Code
-----------------------------

Overlay functions that are ever far called have a stub that jumps to it.
Overlay segmentation and numbering is that in the executable and assigned by
IDA; the actual code segments during play will vary.

Segment 18F2 (IDA overlay 1): (GAME.OVR - add 8 for offset)
Out-of-memory error handling.
Begin End
0000 001C "WARNING: Insufficient Memory"
001D 0041 "Insufficient Memory. Exiting Program"
0042 00AB HeapError handler
          Stub: 0016:0025
          Parameter: uint16 count of bytes that could not be allocated
          (Prints error, then allows one last chance to save the game before
          exiting to DOS.  The documentation says "TIME TO SAVE YOUR GAME"
          should appear, which only happens in v1.2 or earlier; this is instead
          what happens in v1.3.  There aren't many memory leaks in Pool of
          Radiance, but there is no protection against memory fragmentation,
          which SSI's programmers appear to have been well aware of.  Not
          spending code space, disk space, memory, or programmer time on
          fragmentation protection is probably the right move circa 1990.)
00AC 00BD Assign HeapError to the stub at 0016:0025
          Stub: 0016:0020

Segment 18FE (IDA overlay 2): (GAME.OVR - add D4 for offset)
Introduction.
Begin End
0000 0049 Delay or until key pressed, whichever comes first
          Parameter: uint16 delay in seconds
          (The delay will not work if midnight arrives before the delay should
          end, but a key press should still work.)
004B 005F "scenario created by:"
0060 0069 "tsr inc."
006A 0072 "jim ward"
0073 0097 "david cook,steve winter,mike breault"
0098 00A8 "game created by:"
00A9 00BD "ssi special projects"
00BE 00C9 "version 1.3"
00CA 00D6 "programming:"
00D7 00DF "original"
00E0 00EC "scot bayless"
00EF 00F7 "brad myers"
00F8 0103 "keith brors"
0104 010E "russ brown"
010F 0118 "ted greer"
0119 0126 "graphic arts:"
0127 0138 "encounter coding:"
0139 0141 "tom wahl"
0142 014D "paul murray"
014E 0158 "fred butts"
0159 0166 "darla marasco"
0167 0174 "victor penman"
0175 0183 "susan hableib"
0184 0190 "dave shelley"

01A1 01AB "developer:"
01AC 01BD "george mac donald"
01BE 01C6 "testing:"
01C7 01E1 "joel billings,steve salyer"
01E2 0205 "james kucera,robert daly,rick white"
0206 0620 Credits
          (Prints the above Strings.)
0621 0626 "title"
0627 0808 Introduction
          Stub: 0019:0025
          (There are three screens: SSI Presents, etc.; Pool of Radiance, etc.;
          credits.  Only the first is displayed if you have full intro off.
          The second screen flashes the pool, but only if you are using EGA
          graphics; that will also burn RNG seeds the other two graphics modes
          don't.)
0809 080D Empty function
          Stub: 0019:0020

Segment 197F (IDA overlay 3): (GAME.OVR - add 9AC for offset)
Copy protection.
Begin End
0000 0000 ""
0001 0026 "USE THE TRANSLATION WHEEL TO DECIPHER"
0027 0035 "THE CODE WORD."
0036 0059 "MATCH THE ESPRUAR RUNE (OUTER RING)"
005A 007B "WITH THE DETHEK RUNE (INNER RING)"
007C 00A2 "AND READ THE CODE WORD UNDER THE PATH:"
00A3 00A8 "     "
00A9 00CD "READ FROM THE INSIDE TO THE OUTSIDE."
00CE 00E3 "INPUT THE CODE WORD: "
00E4 00FC "Sorry, that's incorrect."
00FD 0358 Copy protection
          Stub: 001C:0025
          (This is about as simple as copy protection code gets; the only
          tricky part about cracking the protection is to do it in a way that
          precisely preserves game behavior.  That typically only matters for
          very deep analysis, including both speedrunning and what you're
          currently reading.  The typical pirate crack often doesn't have that
          kind of precision; the GOG versions I've seen are applied properly.)
0359 035D Empty function
          Stub: 001C:0020

Segment 19B5 (IDA overlay 4): (GAME.OVR - add D46 for offset)
ECL opcode handling.  ECL operands are not normal function parameters.
Begin End
0000 00F3 ECL opcode 00 EXIT
00F4 0110 ECL opcode 01 GOTO
          Operand: GBVM address
          (ECL IP is moved to operand address.)
0111 0125 ECL opcode 02 GOSUB
          Operand: GBVM address
          (Pushes current GBVM address to ECL stack, then moves ECL IP to
          operand address.)
0126 01A2 ECL opcode 03 COMPARE
          Operand 1: variable 1
          Operand 2: variable 2
          (Performs String comparison if operands are both type 128 or 129,
          else performs integer comparison after casting to uint16.  The
          results are stored in DS:6F78...6F7D.)
01A3 0233 ECL opcode arithmetic (04 ADD, 05 SUBTRACT, 06 DIVIDE, 07 MULTIPLY)
          Operand 1: integer
          Operand 2: integer
          Operand 3: GBVM address
          (Operands 1 and 2 are cast to uint16.  Subtrahend comes first for
          SUBTRACT; DIVIDE does not store the remainder as all later Gold Box
          games do, nor does it check for division by zero which should cause
          INT 00 to trip.  Result is stored at operand 3 GBVM address as
          uint16.)
0234 0291 ECL opcode 08 RANDOM
          Operand 1: uint8
          Operand 2: GBVM address
          (Operand 1 is incremented with a maximum of 255, then 0AF8:11C0 is
          called with that value; the return value is written to the GBVM
          address.  For some reason this integer is cast to Real and then
          immediately back to integer.)
0292 02DF ECL opcode 09 SAVE
          Operand 1: variable to save
          Operand 2: GBVM address
          (If operand 1 is type 128, written to GBVM as string; else, written
          to GBVM as its value.)
02E0 03B3 ECL opcode 0A LOAD CHARACTER
          Operand: uint index
03B4 044A ECL opcode 0C SETUP MONSTER
          Operand 1: uint8 monster ID
          Operand 2: uint8 distance (0...2)
          Operand 3: uint8 graphic ID
          (As opcode description.)
044B 044F "CPIC"
0450 0816 ECL opcode 0B LOAD MONSTER
          Operand 1: uint8 monster ID
          Operand 2: uint8 monster count
          Operand 3: uint8 graphic ID
          (Loads the requested combat graphic ID into the next available
          icon animation.  Loads <count> copies of the monster into the
          creature list, with inventory and effect list for each, and with the
          correct icon animation index.)
0817 0850 ECL opcode 0D APPROACH
0851 0851 ""
0852 0855 "PIC"
0856 0950 ECL opcode 0E PICTURE
          Operand: uint8 picture ID
          (* If picture ID is 255:
             End the graphical display and resumes the normal first-person
             view.
           * If GBVM $6DE1 is not 255:
             Display a composite head and body graphic, using $6DE1 as the
             head in HEAD?.DAX and <pictureID> as the body in BODY?.DAX.
           * If GBVM $6DE1 is 255:
             Load the specified picture from PIC?.DAX in the first-person view
             as animation data, then display and animate it.)
0951 0991 ECL opcode 0F INPUT NUMBER
          Operand 1: uint8 digit maximum
          Operand 2: GBVM address
          (Input that cannot be parsed as a uint16 is retried.  Valid input is
          then written to the Operand 2 address.  Operand 1 is ignored; the
          input maximum is always 6.)
0992 0993 " "
0994 0A08 ECL opcode 10 INPUT STRING
          Operand 1: uint8 maximum length
          Operand 2: GBVM address
          (No entry is converted to " ", then the String is written to the
          Operand 2 address.  Operand 1 is ignored; the input maximum length
          is always 40.)
0A09 0A9E ECL opcode print (11 PRINT, 12 PRINTCLEAR)
          Operand: string
          Clears message box if PRINTCLEAR, then prints string in message box,
          witn line break handling.
0A9F 0AEC ECL opcode 13 RETURN
          (Pops the top GBVM return address from the top of the GBVM call stack
          and sets the ECL IP there; acts as no-op if the top of the call stack
          is nil.)
0AED 0B51 ECL opcode 14 COMPARE AND
          Operands 1, 2, 3, 4: values
          (Always sets IF <, IF >, IF <=. and IF >= all to False.  Sets IF = to
          True and IF <> to False if operand 1 equals operand 2 and operand 3
          equals operand 4; otherwise sets IF = to False and IF <> to True.)
0B52 0BC7 ECL opcode comparison (16 IF =, 17 IF <>,
                                 18 IF <, 19 IF >, 1A IF <=, 1B IF >=)
          (Calls 1FDC:1EA6 to advance ECL IP past the next command, unless the
          most recent COMPARE, COMPARE AND, GETTABLE, AND, OR, or FIND ITEM had
          a true comparison.)
0BC8 0BCB "ECL"
0BCD 0BD0 ".dax"
0BD1 0C6C ECL opcode 20 NEWECL
          Operand: uint8 ECL script ID to load
          (Writes previous ECL script ID to $49F2, performs ECL initialization,
          then returns control to the player.  Some time later results in a
          call to 31B4 to complete the process.)
0C6D 0C73 "BACPAC"
0C74 0DEE ECL opcode load files (21 LOAD FILES, 37 LOAD PIECES)
          Operand 1: uint8 file ID
          Operand 2: variable 1
          Operand 3: variable 2
          (As opcode description.)
0DEF 0E5D ECL opcode bitwise (2F AND, 30 OR)
          Operand 1: variable 1
          Operand 2: variable 2
          Operand 3: GBVM address
0E5E 0EBB ECL opcode 2A GETTABLE
          Operand 1: GBVM address 1
          Operand 2: uint index
          Operand 3: GBVM address 2
          (Writes the value of GBVM $(address 1 + index) to address 2.)
0EBC 0F07 ECL opcode 35 SAVE TABLE
          Operand 1: int value
          Operand 2: GBVM address
          Operand 3: uint index
          (Writes value to GBVM $(address + index).)
0F08 1084 ECL opcode 15 VERTICAL MENU
          Operand 1: GBVM address
          Operand 2: message
          Operand 3: uint count of menu choice strings
          Operand 4+: strings
          (Player selects from displayed menu choices in message window; index
          of selection placed at GBVM address.)

          Operand 1: GBVM address
          Operand 2: uint count of menu choice strings

125D 12C5 ECL opcode 1C CLEARMONSTERS
          (Resets some creature flags and clears treasure, freeing dynamically
          allocated heap memory used by all items in the treasure item list.
          Does not delete creatures from the creature list.)

          Operand: GBVM address
          (Writes Party Strength by the calculation in that section to operand

          Writes the 4 values to the 4 GBVM addresses.
1435 1628 ECL opcode 1E CHECKPARTY

1629 16C8 ECL opcode 22 PARTY SURPRISE
          Operand 1: GBVM address 1 (party modifier to be surprised)
          Operand 2: GBVM address 2 (party modifier to surprise)
          (Writes 0 to both operand addresses.  With a ranger in the party,
          increases the first operand address by 1.  This should also happen
          for the second address, but doesn't.)
16C9 178E ECL opcode 23 SURPRISE
          Operand 1: GBVM address 1 (party modifier to be surprised)
          Operand 2: GBVM address 2 (party modifier to surprise)

          Operand 4: uint8 monster modifier to be surprised by party
          (Works, or more properly doesn't, as opcode description.)
178F 1795 "BACPAC"
1796 179A "ICON"
179B 18F7 ECL opcode 24 COMBAT
          (* If GBVM $6E6C is 1, call 1F62:053E shop main menu.
           * If GBVM $6DE2 is 1, call 1D06:0D09 temple main menu.
           * Otherwise starts combat:
             * Calculate maximum monster distance; adjusts GBVM $6DC1 if the
               monsters have to be closer.
             * Call 21FE:007A combat main.
             * Restore 2D map tile set and icons as needed.
             * Reset DS:84D4 duel flag to 0.

           * Restore DS:49F3 interface state.)

          (Executes the ECL command at the current ECL IP.)

          Parameter: uint16 GBVM address

          until either opcode 00 EXIT or opcode 20 NEWECL is executed.)

31B4 32F7 ECL opcode 20 NEWECL completion handler
32F8 3507 Main game loop
          Stub: 001F:0025
          (* Set DS:49F3 interface state according to value of GBVM $49E6.
           * If a save game wasn't loaded, start a new game by loading ECL
             script 0.
           * Call 1FDC:01B6 ECL initialization.
           * Repeat until total party kill:
             * Call 2E68:0989 map main menu
             * If "E"ncamp command issued, call 19B5:2DD3 camp
             * If "L"ook command issued:
               * Backup GBVM $6DCA bit 1 to DS:84DD
               * Set GBVM $6DCA to 1
               * Call 44C1:016D display map view
               * Call ECL script search_location
               * If DS:442F set, call 19B5:31B4 NEWECL completion handler
               * Restore GBVM $6DCA from DS:84DD
             * If movement command issued:
               * Call ECL script vm_run_1
               * Copy current coordinates to GBVM $49F0 and $49F1
               * Call 2E68:0D6D resolve movement command
               * If movement occurred, play sound effect 10
               * Call ECL script search_location
               * If DS:442F set, call 19B5:31B4 NEWECL completion handler.)
3508 350C Empty function
          Stub: 001F:0020

          Parameter: Pointer to int32 experience share

          (* If GBVM $6DE6 mirror duel flag set:

           * Otherwise:
             Add monster loot to global treasure variables and calculates
             experience.  Monster loot is not added if:
             * monster was not hostile
             * monster ran away
             * (items only) GBVM $6DE3 is set to 1
             * (items only) 70% of the time if item has value of 0.)

          (Awards bonuses for single-classed characters with high stats, or
          divides experience by number of classes.)

          (* If normal combat won:
             * Award experience to characters who are Okay
             * Add defeated monster money and items to loot
             * Reset Running characters to Okay and back in play
             * Dying characters stabilized and are now Unconscious
             * Unconscious characters with positive HP rise and are now Okay
           * If normal combat lost:
             * All characters are permanently lost
             * Party size at GBVM $6E3E reset to 0
           * If duel:
             * Winning participants receive duel-specific experience, no loot
             * Resets characters flagged as out of play for the duel back to in
             * Dying characters stabilized and are now Unconscious
             * Does not end game if lost

             * Reset Running characters to Okay and back in play
             * All other characters are permanently lost immediately

          uninitialized stack variable.  If special read handling from GBVM
          zone 1 is triggered, the GBVM is usually completely ignored.)
0F53 1152 Write String to GBVM address
          Stub: 002B:005C
          Parameter 1: uint16 GBVM address (must be in GBVM zones 0...3)
          Parameter 2: Pointer to String
          (Strings are copied to the GBVM as C strings; exception: GBVM address
          $6B00, character name, which is copied directly to character data as
          a normal Pascal String but not to the GBVM.  Byte size is always
          standard for that zone otherwise: 16-bit bytes for zones 0...2, 8-bit
          bytes for zone 3.)

          happen.)

          Parameter: Pointer to Creature
          Return in AL: Boolean item used
          (Rolls 1d7 for the lowest AI priority to consider.  Loops first from
          7 to lowest AI priority, then through inventory; if any item is
          found that is high enough AI priority, the AI attempts to use that
          item.)

          Parameter: Pointer to Creature
          Return in AL: Boolean spell cast
          (Rolls 1d7 for the lowest AI priority to consider.  Loops first from
          7 to lowest AI priority, then through 4 memorized spells chosen at
          random; if any spell is found that is high enough AI priority, the AI
          attempts to cast that spell.)

          Parameter: Pointer to Creature
          Return in AL: Boolean True
          (* Call 00BA:2637 end creature actions this combat round, if any
             of the following apply:
             * Creature is helpless
             * Creature has a ranged weapon equipped
             * Creature has initiative 0
           * Call 00BA:2686 creature guard, otherwise.)

          Parameter: Pointer to Creature

128D 13C9 Combat AI calculate weapon priority
          Parameter 1: Pointer to Creature
          Parameter 2: Pointer to Item
          Return in AL: uint8 weapon priority
          (* Special cases checked for that always return 0:
             * Item requires too many hands for creature to equip
             * Item is aligned weapon that does not match creature
             * Javelin of Lightning
             * Item is cursed, even if the item is not identified
           * Start with small/medium weapon dice multiplied by small/medium
             weapon sides.
           * Add 8 multiplied by any positive magical bonus.
           * Add 2 multiplied by any positive small/medium weapon bonus.
           * Set to 8 if item is Holy Water and other creature is vulnerable to
             turning.
           * If bit 8 of weapon bitmask set, add 2 * (rate of fire x2 - 1).
           * Add 3 if weapon is one-handed.
          Large weapon damage is not considered.)
13CA 1921 Combat AI equip weapon and shield
          Parameter: Pointer to Creature
          (* Calculate highest ranged weapon priority by call to 128D.
           * Calculate highest melee weapon priority by call to 128D; the
             default melee weapon is bare hands, with a weapon priority of 2.
           * Calculate highest shield priority as highest non-negative.
           * If highest ranged weapon priority is a bow without arrows, or a
             crossbow without bolts, ranged weapons are not considered.
           * Defaults to melee weapon if:
             * there is no ranged weapon to consider; or
             * highest ranged weapon priority is less than or equal to half of
               highest melee weapon priority
           * If highest ranged weapon priority is a throwable melee weapon, it
             is chosen.
           * Otherwise, the best melee weapon is chosen if there are any
             enemies within melee range, else the best ranged weapon is chosen.
           * Changing weapons now happens, but only if the current equipped
             weapon is both a different weapon and is not cursed.
           * If it is possible to equip a better shield than any current
             shield, that also now happens.)
1922 1926 Empty function
          Stub: 0038:0020

Effect handling.  Most of these functions are invoked via the function pointer
array at DS:682C, which has 139 entries; these in turn are usually called from
4208:02FA trigger effects with condition.  All such effect handlers have the
following signature:
          Parameter 1: Pointer to Creature
          Parameter 2: Pointer to Item *or* Effect
          Parameter 3: Boolean item unreadied *or* effect being removed
Begin End
0000 001E Nullify attack with effect ID
          Parameter: uint8 effect ID
          (If effect ID 0, check always succeeds.  Otherwise checks ID against
          current effect to check for; if they match, both damage and effect
          to check for are set to 0.)

          Parameter 1: Pointer to Creature
          Parameter 2: uint8 effect ID
          Parameter 3: uint8 effect strength
          Parameter 4: uint16 minutes remaining of effect

          lock flag is not on.  The parameter order is not the same.  Always
          sets effect removal calls effect handler to True.)

          Parameter 2: Pointer to defending Creature
          Parameter 3: Boolean is from behind
          Parameter 4: Pointer to Pointer to Boolean creature done this round
          Return at Pointer: Boolean creature done this round
          (* If defender is helpless:
             * Play sound effect 7
             * Increment current number of attacks by 1
             * Automatically does enough damage to set defender HP to -5
             * Set all attacker remaining attacks to 0
             * Set attacker done this round
             * Remove attacker invisibility
           * If defender is not helpless:
             * Determine if Large weapon damage should be done; sets attacker
               damage if approrpriate
             * Trigger defender Armor Class effects
             * Determine whether attack is from behind; attack is considered
               from behind if either this check passes or caller says so
             * Determine whether attack is backstab
             * Apply any penalties for a ranged attack
             * Deduct 1 from attacker remaining attacks
             * Increment current number of attacks by 1
             * Remove attacker invisibility
             * Perform attack roll; if attack hits:
               * Play sound effect 7
               * Call 0191 calculate weapon damage
               * Call 02F9 process attack result
               * Trigger attacker weapon damage effects
             * Repeat until defender reduced to 0 HP, or attacker exhausts
               attacks remaining
             * If no attacks hit:
               * Play sound effect 9
               * Display message saying attacker missed
               * Set attacker done this round.)
1B1E 1BD7 Combat modify defense for distance
          Parameter 1: Pointer to attacking Creature
          Parameter 2: Pointer to defending Creature
          Parameter 3: Pointer to uint8 defender 60 minus AC
          Return in Pointer: modified defender 60 minus AC
          (No effect for any melee attack.  For a ranged attack, subrange is
          item range minus 1 then divided by 3.  Any attack more than 1
          subrange away adds 2 to defense; any attack more than 2 subranges
          away adds 3 to defense.)

          Stub: 0068:0061
          Parameter 1: Pointer to active Creature
          Parameter 2: Pointer to Pointer to target Creature
          Return in AL: Boolean if any appropriate target found
          (Target creature must be no more than 1 tile away from active
          creature.  Checks all nine directions.
           * Searches for all conscious creatures who have less than maximum
             hit points and have the same hostility flag.  Active creature is
             itself highest priority if at less than half maximum hit points,
             else highest priority is whoever has the fewest hit points.
           * Also searches for unconscious creatures within range.  I don't see
             any logic stopping a hostile monster from healing an unconscious
             character.  Highest priority is whichever unconscious creature is
             checked last; the order is north, northeast, ..., northwest, same
             square.
           * Conscious characters are higher priority than unconscious ones
             if they have fewer than 7 hit points.)

0699 069D ".spc"
069E 0AC3 Save character
          Stub: 007C:0034
          Parameter 1: Pointer to character
          Parameter 2: Pointer to file name String (without extension)
          (* Step 1:
             * If writing a single character, update CHARLIST.TXT and construct
               filename using sanitized character name.
             * If writing for a saved game, use supplied file name.
           * Step 2: destination file is file name + ".cha" or ".str"
             The file is deleted if it exists; then the full 285-byte character
             record is written as-is; no pointers are removed.
           * Step 3: destination file is file name + ".itm"
             The file is deleted if it exists; then all 63-byte item records
             are written as-is, one after another; no pointers are removed.
             If there are no items, the file won't exist.
           * Step 4: destination file is file name + ".spc"
             The file is deleted if it exists; then all 9-byte effect records
             are written as-is, one after another; no pointers are removed.
             If there are no effects, the file won't exist.)

0AD6 0AD7 ":"

0AE7 0AEB ".spc"

0000 02F1 Time out effects
          Parameter 1: uint8 time count
          Parameter 2: uint8 time unit (1 = minute, 2 = turn, 3 = hour,
            4 = day, 5 = month; 0 = segment and 6 = year are recognized but
            are never used and will not work)
          (Times out effects by the specific duration.  Any effects that are
          triggered by being removed will still trigger normally.  Any
          dependency on precise timing will not work, steps can be by as much
          as 10 minutes.  The logic here is strange-looking but I've never seen
          problems in practice.)

          Parameter: Pointer to GBVM clock component array
          (* Fixes array so no component exceeds its limit; e.g. if the array
             has 65 minutes, that changes to 5 minutes and 1 hour is added.
           * Ages your characters, but only after 256 years have elapsed
             because of a bug.)

Segment 3E45 (IDA overlay 23): (GAME.OVR - add 27018 for offset)
Spell.  Many of these are invoked via the function pointer array at DS:6B84,
which has 68 entries, and which is invoked from 0C20.  Spell function pointers
(aside from the special index 0 function) have no arguments and return nothing,
and depend on global variables for what normally would be arguments.  The
current creature is always the caster.

          67; defaults to spell table otherwise.)

          Parameter 1: uint8 spell ID

            bit 128 can be hostility flag)
          Parameter 3: Boolean does effect removal call handler
          Parameter 4: uint8 damage
          Parameter 5: uint8 element bitmask
          Parameter 6: Pointer to String
          (Current creature is the caster; target creatures must be defined in
          global variables.  For each target creature:
           * Roll for saving throw and attack, as needed.  Spells requiring an
             attack roll trigger target creature Armor Class effects.
           * Apply saving throw success result to damage.
           * Apply any caused effect to target creature.
           * Reset DS:6819 element bitmask to 0.)

SECTION 12.6.4 - Data Segment
-----------------------------

This assumes you are using v1.3.  The following is not comprehensive, although
it's approaching that.

All provided addresses are in hexadecimal and are their location in the data
segment (DS).  Gold Box Virtual Machine addresses and DS offsets are *not* the
same, though I'd expect their equivalents to be same running on the original
6502 (not x86) architecture.

More complicated data structures are provided in the Data Structures section.

Executable addresses: These are loaded before any machine code executes and are
present in the executable.  Anything declared by a programmer should not change
unless the programmer does so (most of these are constant), but Turbo Pascal
default global variables can (like errno in C).  To convert to the location in
the decompressed executable, add 0xD0A0.

Begin End
0000 0001 Pointed to by the nil near data pointer; should always be 0.  In most
          modern operating systems/programming langauges, attempting to
          dereference nil causes a fatal error; in DOS programs it does not,
          but for DS should only be done to check if some misbehaving code has
          put something here.  Not present before v1.3, so that's likely new
          intelligence on the compiler's part; the compiler does not print any
          error message before exiting if this is not 0, unlike more recent DOS
          compilers.
0002 0112 Copy protection array; size 13, 21 bytes each.
0113 0113 (byte alignment)
0114 0119 Effect IDs removed by Cure Disease, 1-indexed; size 6, 1 byte each.
011A 02B3 Temple spell String array; size 10, 41 bytes each.
02B4 02F1 (unknown)
02F2 02F5 Map direction to combat direction mapping array, 0-indexed; size 4, 1
          byte each.
02F6 0341 (unknown)
0342 0381 Gnome THAC0 bonus monster String array, 1-indexed; size 4, 16 bytes
          each.
0382 0401 Dwarf THAC0 bonus monster String array, 1-indexed; size 8, 16 bytes
          each.
0402 0481 Dwarf/gnome AC bonus vs. giant monster string array, 1-indexed; size
          8 (last 3 entries are empty), 16 bytes each.
0482 04B5 Turn undead table, by undead type then by cleric level, 1-indexed;
          size 10*10, 1 byte each.  Table only exists down to Vampire;
          0 = destroyed, -1 = 6+1d6 destroyed.
04B6 04DE 3D map exploration menu.
04DF 0507 2D map exploration menu.
0508 0530 Camp main menu String.
0531 0559 Camp magic menu String.
055A 0583 Camp alter menu String.
0584 05AC Camp order menu String array, 0-indexed; size 2, 41 bytes each.
05AD 05D5 (unknown)
05D6 05DD Item usability bit by class array, 0-indexed; size 8, 1 byte each.
05DE 05E5 Basic class bitmask by class array, 0-indexed; size 8, 1 byte each.
05E6 05EF (unknown)
05F0 0618 Main menu option String.
0619 07E6 Main menu option record array; size 11, 42 bytes each.  The last byte
          in each option is a Boolean that flags whether the option is valid
          (and may be changed).
07E7 07E7 (byte alignment)
07E8 08B4 Create character icon menu option string array, 1-indexed; size 5, 41
          bytes each.
08B5 08B5 (byte alignment)
08B6 08BD Starting HP dice by class array, 0-indexed; size 8, 1 byte each.
08BE 08C5 Starting HP sides by class array, 0-indexed; size 8, 1 byte each.
08C6 08C7 Character icon animation height marker array, 1-indexed; size 2, 1
          byte each.
08C8 08D7 NPC random head ID array, 0/1-indexed; size 2*8, 1 byte each.
          (The first dimension for these five arrays is gender.)
08D8 08E1 NPC cleric random body ID array, 0/1-indexed; size 2*5, 1 byte each.
08E2 08EB NPC fighter random body ID array, 0/1-indexed; size 2*5, 1 byte each.
08EC 08F5 NPC MU random body ID array, 0/1-indexed; size 2*5, 1 byte each.
08F6 08FF NPC thief random body ID array, 0/1-indexed; size 2*5, 1 byte each.
0900 090A Unknown Yarash's Pyramid array, 0-indexed; size 11, 1 byte each.
090B 091C Unknown Yarash's Pyramid array, 0-indexed; size 18, 1 byte each.
091D 092C Unknown Yarash's Pyramid array; size 16, 1 byte each.
092D 093C Unknown Yarash's Pyramid array; size 16, 1 byte each.
093D 094C Unknown Yarash's Pyramid array; size 16, 1 byte each.
094D 095E Unknown Yarash's Pyramid array, 0-indexed; size 9, 2 bytes each.
095F 0970 Unknown Yarash's Pyramid array, 0-indexed; size 9, 2 bytes each
0971 0971 (byte alignment)
0972 0AA1 Random item array; size 19, 16 bytes each.
0AA2 0C13 Spell level String array, 1-indexed; size 9, 41 bytes each.
0C14 0C23 Effect IDs to remove when reduced to 0 HP, 1-indexed; size 16, 1 byte
          each.
0C24 0C25 Effect IDs to remove with unknown trigger, 1-indexed; size 2, 1 byte
          each.
0C26 0C35 Sky/ceiling color map, 0-indexed; size 16, 1 byte each.
0C36 0C65 Wilderness map special location array, 1-indexed; size 9, 6 bytes
          each.
0C6C 0C7B Unknown animation data array; size 16, 1 byte each.
0C7C 0C8B Unknown animation data array; size 16, 1 byte each.
0C8C 0CD1 (unknown)
0CD2 0CE1 Unknown icon animation array; size 16, 1 byte each.
0CE2 0CF1 Unknown icon animation array; size 16, 1 byte each.
0CF2 0D01 Unknown icon animation array; size 16, 1 byte each.
0D02 0D15 (unknown)
0D16 0D19 Set of punctuation for splitting up String; 32...63 only, 4 bytes.
0D1A 0D23 Filename invalid char array, 1-indexed; size 10, 1 byte each.
0D24 0D37 Coin valuation in copper array; size 5, 4 bytes each.
0D38 0F02 Class String array, 0-indexed; size 17, 27 bytes each.
0F03 0F52 Race String array, 0-indexed; size 8, 10 bytes each.
0F53 0FEB Alignment String array, 0-indexed; size 9, 17 bytes each.
0FEC 0FF9 Gender String array, 0-indexed; size 2, 7 bytes each.
0FFA 1046 Money String array, 0-indexed; size 7, 11 bytes each.
1047 10BA Status String array, 0-indexed; size 9, 13 bytes each.
10BB 10BB (byte alignment)
10BC 25A6 Item name component String array, 1-indexed; size 255, 21 bytes each.
25A7 25A7 (byte alignment)
25A8 25BF Compass direction String array, 0-indexed; size 8, 3 bytes each.
25C0 25C8 Direction by Y/X-axis delta 2D array, -1/-1-indexed; size 3*3, 1 byte
          each.
25C9 25CC EGA font bitplane bitmask array, 0-indexed; size 4, 1 byte each.
25CD 25D4 CGA font color bitmask array, 0-indexed; size 4, 2 bytes each.
25D5 25E4 Font 16-color EGA to 4-color CGA mapping array; size 16, 1 byte each.
25E5 25F4 CGA position bitmask array; size 8, 2 bytes each.
25F5 25F5 (byte alignment)
25F6 25F9 EGA pixel bitplane bitmask array; size 4, 1 byte each.
25FA 2609 Color then column mapping to CGA value 2D array, 0-indexed; size 4*4,
          1 byte each.
260A 2611 (unknown)
2612 2631 Sound effect array; size 16, 2 bytes each.  It's just a table of
          int16 from -1...14.
2632 2721 "Insert disk # in drive " array, 1-indexed; size 8, 30 bytes each.
2722 272B Subtrahends to convert 8x8 symbol number to 8x8 set ID array; size 5,
          2 bytes each.
272C 2735 (unknown)
2736 273E X-axis delta by direction array, 0-indexed; size 9, 1 byte each.
273F 2747 Y-axis delta by direction array, 0-indexed, size 9, 1 byte each.
2748 284B Combat unknown 1 array, 1-indexed; size 65, 4 bytes each.
284C 286B Icon coordinate delta array, 1/0-indexed; size 4*4, 2 bytes each;
          unused coordinates are represented by (-1,-1).
286C 286F Helplessness effect ID array, 1-indexed; size 4, 1 byte each.
2870 287D Head ID to DAX ID mapping array, 1-indexed; size 14, 1 byte each.
287E 2889 Body ID to DAX ID mapping array, 1-indexed; size 12, 1 byte each.
288A 288B (unknown; probably unused)
288C 2894 Digit to extended scan code array, 1-indexed; size 9, 1 byte each.
2895 290D (unknown)
290E 2911 2x2 delta array index array, 1-indexed; size 4, 1 byte each.
2912 3209 Spell String array, 1-indexed; size 56, 41 bytes each.
320A 3639 Spell table, 1-indexed; size 67, 16 bytes each.  The first 56 entries
          are same spells given in Creature Format; the next 11 represent
          magical effects 80...90 in Item Format.  Spell ID 0 in some cases is
          the special case of the spell selection UI and is not part of this
          array.
363A 3647 GBVM clock component limit array, 0-indexed; size 7, 2 bytes each.
          This defines a round as 10 segments, but I don't believe that is ever
          directly used.
3648 3C77 Wilderness icon map, 0-indexed; size 36*44, 1 byte each.
3C78 3C7A Wilderness X-coordinate correction array, 2-indexed; size 3, 1 byte
          each.
3C7B 3C7B (byte alignment)
3C7C 3CD3 THAC0 by class then level (0...10) 2D array; size 8*11, 1 byte each.
          THAC0 is represented by (60 - THAC0).
3CD4 3CF5 (unknown)
3CF6 3CFB Default character icon colors, 1-indexed; size 6, 1 byte each.
3CFC 3D43 Thief ability table by level, 1-indexed; size 9, 8 bytes each.
3D44 3D7B Thief ability adjustment table by race, 1-indexed (monster race not
          included); size 7*8, 1 byte each .
3D7C 3DB2 Thief ability adjustment table by Dexterity, 9-indexed; size 11
          (9...19), 5 bytes each.  Any out-of-range Dexterity will result in
          out-of-bounds array values.
3DB3 3E22 Minimum/maximum statistics by race, 1-indexed (monster race not
          included); size 7, 16 bytes each.
3E23 3E76 Classes allowed by race array, 0-indexed; size 7, 12 bytes each
          (includes multi-class; monster race not included).  First byte is the
          number of classes, followed by the list of classes, padded with 0.
3E77 3F3A Starting age table, 4 bytes each, 0-indexed; size 7*7, 4 bytes each.
          Grouped by class (excluding monks and multi-class) then race
          (excluding monster).  Multi-classed characters use the highest age.
          Starting age is [byte 0] + [256 * byte 1] + [byte 2]d[byte 3].
3F3B 3F80 Age category table by race, 1-indexed (monster race not included);
          size 7, 10 bytes each .  Each entry is an array of 5 16-bit integers
          with the maximum age for each of the 5 age categories.
3F81 3FE6 Minimum statistics by class table (including multi-class); size 17, 6
          bytes each.
3FE7 4090 Alignments allowed by class table (including multi-class); size 17,
          10 bytes each.  First byte is the number of alignments, followed
          by the list of alignments, padded with 0.
4091 40A2 Constitution bonus hit point for non-warriors array, 3-indexed; size
          18 (3...20), 1 byte each.  Any Constitution outside of this will read
          out-of-bounds array values (see Statistic: Constitution for some).
40A3 4262 Experience and spells by class table, 0-indexed; size 8, 56 bytes
          each.
4263 4272 Starting gold array by class table, 0-indexed; size 8, 2 bytes each.
          First byte is number of dice to roll, second byte is number of sides.
4273 43DA Saving throw table, 0/1-indexed; size 8*9, 5 bytes each.  Arranged
          by class, then by level (1...9).
43DB 43DB (byte alignment)
43DC 43DD OvrResult.  (TPv6PG, p. 174.)
43DE 43DF (unknown)
43E0 43E1 OvrTrapCount.  (TPv6PG, p. 174.)
43E2 43E3 OvrLoadCount.  (TPv6PG, p. 174.)
43E4 43E4 OvrFileMode.  (TPv6PG, p. 174.)
43E5 43E5 (byte alignment)
43E6 43E7 Size of overlay probationary area, in units of 16 bytes.  (Not used
          by Pool of Radiance.)
43E8 43EB Overlay file position.
43EC 43ED OvrCodeList.  (TPv6PG, p. 137.)
43EE 43EF OvrHeapSize.  (TPv6PG, p. 137.)
43F0 43F3 OvrDebugPtr. (TPv6PG, p. 137; points to 0AA2:02E5).
43F4 43F5 OvrHeapOrg.  (TPv6PG, p. 210.)
43F6 43F7 OvrHeapPtr.  (TPv6PG, p. 137.)
