SECTION 12.6.5 - Code Hacking
-----------------------------

This section mostly presumes you are modifying v1.3.  All of these changes are
straightforward and small, but none have been truly tested; experiment at your
own risk.  The intent here is as much to demonstrate how to make changes as to
actually suggest them.

Any disassembly is from IDA Pro and uses its conventions.  Locations vary with
game version; the listed ones are for v1.3 unless otherwise specified.

Removing the copy protection
  This should work for any version:

  ; GAME.OVR in 197F:00FD copy protection
    1E                push    ds
    57                push    di
  ; The below xx xx xx xx varies with version (string comparison library call):
  ;    24 07 2E 0C in v1.2
  ;    10 0C F8 0A in v1.3
    9A xx xx xx xx    call    sub_BB90
    75 02             jnz     short loc_19B20
  ; ^^^^^ GOG patch: replace with 90 90 (nop / nop)
  ;        my patch: replace with 74 02 (jz short loc_19B20)
    EB 25             jmp     short loc_19B45

  If you have a version for which the above does not work (this solution is
  inelegant but always works):
  (1) Open START.EXE or ST.EXE, whichever you have
  (2) Search for this text string: "BEWARE"
  (3) Replace this text and all subsequent 6-letter code words with "XXXXXX"
      (or any other all-capitals 6-letter string of your choice).  There should
      be 13 of these, with the last one being "WYVERN".
  You can now enter your 6-letter string instead of having to use the code
  wheel.

  Any of these fixes should precisely preserve original game behavior, which
  then-contemporary cracks often do not; completely suppressing any randomized
  documentation question changes RNG seeding.  That only matters for either
  in-depth analysis like this, or stunt runs and speed runs.  (It's good to
  know that GOG does it properly.)

  My change to GOG's patch only matters if you want to see what happens if you
  enter a wrong answer, by entering a correct answer.  That doesn't really
  matter here, but it does in games which have entertaining responses to copy
  protection failures, like Curse of the Azure Bonds.

Paralysis spell saving throw fixes
  To make the three paralysis spells use the correct saving throws:

  ; START.EXE in DS:320A spell array
  ; Change 1: change cleric Hold Person to save vs. paralysis
    00 02 06 00 04 01 06 00 01 04 34 01 05 07 01 00
  ;                            ^^ change 04 to 00

  ; Change 2: change magic-user Hold Person to save vs. paralysis
    01 03 0C 00 00 02 07 00 01 04 34 01 03 06 01 00
  ;                            ^^ change 04 to 00

  ; Change 3: change Wand of Paralyzation to save vs. rod/staff/wand
    02 06 06 00 00 00 04 00 01 00 34 01 00 07 01 00
  ;                            ^^ change 00 to 02

  This won't fix the Wand of Fireballs or Wand of Lightning Bolt.

ECL opcode 23 SURPRISE fix
  To apply surprise modifiers correctly:

  ; GAME.OVR in 19B5:16C9 ECL opcode 23 SURPRISE
    8A 46 F8          mov     al, [bp+var_8]
    30 E4             xor     ah, ah
    8B D0             mov     dx, ax
    8A 46 FB          mov     al, [bp+var_5]
  ;       ^^ change to FA (mov al, [bp+var_6])
    30 E4             xor     ah, ah
    40                inc     ax
    40                inc     ax
    2B C2             sub     ax, dx
    88 46 F7          mov     [bp+var_9], al
    8A 46 FA          mov     al, [bp+var_6]
  ;       ^^ change to FB (mov al, [bp+var_5])
    30 E4             xor     ah, ah
    8B D0             mov     dx, ax

ECL opcode 34 ECL CLOCK fix
  This should make time pass properly (in minutes, up to 255), if you change
  a script to add this opcode (this will have no effect otherwise):

  ; GAME.OVR in 19B5:2AC1 ECL opcode 34 ECL CLOCK
    B0 01             mov     al, 1
    50                push    ax
    9A 2A 00 2B 00    call    sub_2DA
    8A 46 FF          mov     al, [bp+var_1]
  ; ^^^^^^^^ change to B0 01 90 (mov al, 1 / nop); var_1 is not initialized
    50                push    ax
    9A 25 00 2B 00    call    sub_2D5

ECL opcode 3B SPELL fix
  This doesn't work because GBVM addresses are cast to uint8.  To fix in the
  one place where this opcode gets used:

  ; GAME.OVR in 19B5:2C03 ECL opcode 3B SPELL
  ; Change #1: read GBVM addresses correctly
    55                push    bp
    89 E5             mov     bp, sp
    83 EC 0A          sub     sp, 0Ah
  ;       ^^ change to 0C (to reserve 2 extra bytes on the stack)
    B0 03             mov     al, 3
    50                push    ax
    9A 2A 00 2B 00    call    sub_2DA
    B0 01             mov     al, 1
    50                push    ax
    9A 25 00 2B 00    call    sub_2D5
    88 46 FF          mov     [bp+var_1], al
    A0 87 70          mov     al, byte_13846+1
    50                push    ax
    A0 47 70          mov     al, byte_13806+1
    50                push    ax
    9A 4D 00 2B 00    call    sub_2FD
    88 46 F7          mov     [bp+var_9], al
  ; ^^^^^^^^ change to 89 46 F6 (mov [bp+var_A], ax)
    A0 88 70          mov     al, byte_13846+2
    50                push    ax
    A0 48 70          mov     al, byte_13806+2
    50                push    ax
    9A 4D 00 2B 00    call    sub_2FD
    88 46 F6          mov     [bp+var_A], al
  ; ^^^^^^^^ change to 89 46 F4 (mov [bp+var_C], ax)

  ; Change #2: write to GBVM addresses correctly
    8A 46 F7          mov     al, [bp+var_9]
  ; ^^^^^^^^ change to 8B 46 F6 (mov ax, [bp+var_A])
    30 E4             xor     ah, ah
  ; ^^^^^ change all to 90 (nop)
    50                push    ax
    8A 46 FE          mov     al, [bp+var_2]
    30 E4             xor     ah, ah
    50                push    ax
    9A 52 00 2B 00    call    sub_302
    8A 46 F6          mov     al, [bp+var_A]
  ; ^^^^^^^^ change to 8B 46 F4 (mov ax, [bp+var_C])
    30 E4             xor     ah, ah
  ; ^^^^^ change all to 90 (nop)
    50                push    ax
    8A 46 FD          mov     al, [bp+var_3]
    30 E4             xor     ah, ah
    50                push    ax
    9A 52 00 2B 00    call    sub_302

Mirror Image fix
  The Mirror Image effect handler checks that a spell is being cast before it
  will function, which is neither how it works in Gold Box games beginning with
  Secret of the Silver Blades, nor is that what the PHB says should happen.  To
  remove this check:

  ; GAME.OVR in 2715:09C0 Mirror Image effect handler
    3C 01             cmp     al, 1
    76 5A             jbe     short loc_27B87
    80 3E 1B 68 00    cmp     byte_12FDB, 0
  ; ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ change all to 90 (nop)
    76 53             jbe     short loc_27B87
  ; ^^^^^ change all to 90 (nop)
    80 3E 20 68 00    cmp     byte_12FE0, 0
    75 4C             jnz     short loc_27B87

Spellcasting delay patch
  Spellcasting delay is more than three times that of tabletop version.  If you
  want to change spellcasting time to be more true to the original rules (this
  will significantly affect game balance, as the balancing features of weapon
  speed delays and additional wands/scrolls/potions aren't present):

  ; GAME.OVR in 2AA0:2414 combat cast spell
    30 E4             xor     ah, ah
    99                cwd
    B9 03 00          mov     cx, 3
  ;    ^^ change 03 to 01 (or whatever other divisor you prefer)
    F7 F1             div     cx

Random item generation fixes
  To fix some of the gibberish items you can get with random items:

  ; GAME.OVR in 3C1F:1010 generate random item
  ; Change #1: fix for Javelin of Lightning
    88 46 FF          mov     [bp+var_1], al
    80 7E FF 05       cmp     byte ptr [bp+var_1], 5
    75 04             jnz     short loc_3D2C9
    C6 46 FC 90       mov     byte ptr [bp+var_4], 90h
  ;          ^^ change 90 to 69 (105 = Javelin of Lightning)

  ; Change #2: fix for Wand of Lightning
    B0 01             mov     al, 1
    50                push    ax
    B0 03             mov     al, 3
    50                push    ax
    9A 48 00 B0 00    call    sub_B48
    30 E4             xor     ah, ah
    40                inc     ax
  ; ^^ change 40 to 90 (nop); changes 1d3+1 to 1d3
    88 46 FF          mov     [bp+var_1], al
    8A 46 FF          mov     al, [bp+var_1]

  ; Change #3: fix for potion generation
    3C 47             cmp     al, 47h
    75 41             jnz     short loc_3D9CC
    B0 01             mov     al, 1
    50                push    ax
    B0 0B             mov     al, 0Bh
  ;    ^^ change 0B to 0A; changes 1d11 to 1d10
    50                push    ax
    9A 48 00 B0 00    call    sub_B48

  This won't fix the blank items you sometimes get, or enable Gauntlets of Ogre
  Power as a random item.


SECTION 13.1 - Technical Bugs
-----------------------------

There is no sharp line between a "technical" bug and an "adaptational" bug;
just wanted to split up the list somehow.

* There are numerous timer bugs where real-life midnight resetting the clock
  is not accounted for.  Often, pressing a key will resume normal behavior, but
  you may need to restart the game.  (This is common in DOS games of the era.
  You often can't use timer interrupts to avoid this, if those are tied up
  handling sound.  You *can* check the date, but that typically isn't done.)
* Exiting creating a character before the character is fully created causes a
  memory leak.  (This is the only memory leak I've found.)
* The game may crash if you try to create a character with too many chars
  that are invalid in a DOS filename (like "....").
* You can manually lower statistics below their defined minima.
* Strength 19...25 do not allow you to bash down an easy-to-bash door.
* Constitution is only properly defined from 3...20 (and only behaves correctly
  from 4...18).
* Constitution hit point bonuses are not properly calculated for multi-classed
  characters after level 1.
* Any Constitution saving throw bonus versus poison also applies to
  paralyzation/death.
* A character under the effect of Haste or Slow has movement adjusted in the
  wrong direction when viewing the character (actual movement is correct).
* The passage of time does not actually age your characters, until after 256
  years have elapsed; then you age by 1 year every minute.
* Dwarves do not get a to-hit bonus against GOBLIN GUARD (#2), HOBGOBLIN LDR
  (#7), or GRISHNAK (#28).
* Elves do not get a to-hit bonus with a Fine Composite Long Bow.
* Gnomes do not get a to-hit bonus against GOBLIN GUARD (#2).
* Single-target damage spells should not be able to raise the dead.
* Thieving abilities are not necessarily adjusted promptly.
* Charm effect removal may not properly restore a hired NPC.
* Dispel Magic does not (and cannot) check against effect caster level if that
  is not remembered.
* The Shield spell does not protect against a Wand of Magic Missiles.
* Mirror Image does not work against physical attacks, only targeted spells.
  (Fixed in Secret of the Silver Blades.)
* When manipulating coins, coins with a total value above 65535 gold (13107
  platinum) may be lost during a transaction.
* If you sell anything in gold value, it gets converted to platinum and
  rounded down; any gold remainder is lost.
* A Bag of Holding won't cleanly allow you to pick up all the weight you can.
* A Flame Tongue sword has no special effect.  (Fixed in Curse of the Azure
  Bonds.)
* Keoghtum's Ointment should not convert poisoned characters to dead.
* The Manual of Bodily Health attempts to adjust hit points, but doesn't do so
  correctly, in multiple ways.
* A random Sling shouldn't weigh 8 pounds.
* The Wand of Fireballs and Wand of Lightning Bolt require targets to save vs.
  spell, not rod/staff/wand.
* The acid squirt attack of the AHNKHEG (#65) isn't properly marked as
  acid damage.
* GENHEERIS (#88) is unable to memorize spells.
* ACOLYTE (#108) has a race of Monster (so some magic won't work as expected),
  and has 0 XP, which allows the NPC to be "M"odified, which causes
  array-out-of-bounds errors.
* The MUMMY (#114) vulnerability to magical fire is coded as magical *or* fire.
* ECL opcode 22 PARTY SURPRISE doesn't do anything special with no ranger in
  the party.  Even if you do, it doesn't set one of the surprise variables
  properly.
* ECL opcode 23 SURPRISE does not apply the surprise modifiers properly.
* ECL opcode 28 ROB does not correctly restore item chance during iteration of
  a character's inventory.
* ECL opcode 3B SPELL was improperly coded and will never do anything.

SECTION 13.2 - Scripting Bugs
-----------------------------

This doesn't include spelling or grammar errors, which are numerous.

* Civilized Area: you may rest in an inn, even if you have angered Phlan and
  you are told you aren't allowed to.
* Civilized Area: the shop door at (9,14) works at night.
* Slums: the game doesn't properly check the random encounter counter while
  resting, but credits you with a clear point for defeating monsters that
  appear when disturbed, allowing you to clear the block without dealing with
  all set encounters.
* Slums: for set encounters, the game doesn't first check to see if you ran
  away from combat before crediting you with victory.  (This is a common
  scripting error in the Gold Box games.)
* Kuto's Well: if rest is interrupted by monsters, they will be surprised.
* Podal Plaza: there is supposed to be a difference in resting between being on
  mission and not clearing the block, but the latter immediately clobbers the
  former, so it doesn't actually happen.
* Podal Plaza: in a random encounter, running away is only checked for after
  you have been credited with victory.
* Podal Plaza: you can defeat the buccaneer and take his treasure twice.
* Cadorna Textile House: in a random encounter, running away is only checked
  for after you have been credited with victory.
* Cadorna Textile House: rest can be disturbed even if it is safe.
* Cadorna Textile House: the Ring of Feather Falling does not prevent fall
  damage in the well.
* Cadorna Textile House: Restal can open the Cadorna family treasure more than
  once.
* Kovel Mansion: the number of thieves in the location 14 ambush is not
  properly calculated.
* Wealthy Area: you have only one chance to find the treasure at location 4.
* Wealthy Area: going south from (4,15) causes a crash.
* Yarash's Pyramid: completing and leaving the pyramid can incorrectly flag
  the endgame scripts.
* Nomad Camp: the Shaman carries a "Bracers AC 2" that is actually AC 4.
* Lizardman Keep: if you give an incorrect friend-word, the combats at
  locations 3 and 10 aren't correct.
* Lizardman Keep: the count of giant lizards doesn't work correctly.
* Buccaneer Base: you can get the heir to Bivant even if you already have him.
* Zhentil Keep Outpost: you can have dinner with the commandant even if you've
  already fought some of the guards.
* Zhentil Keep Outpost: you aren't supposed to be able to leave the outpost
  from your room before dinner without encountering the commandant.
* Zhentil Keep Outpost: you aren't supposed to fight Al-Hyam Dazid here.
* Valhingen Graveyard: the treasure of locations 12 and 24 are transposed.
* Stojanow Gate: the bugbears become outright hostile if you refuse to pass
  through the gate (they should probably just become more suspicious).
* Stojanow Gate: the bugbears should always become hostile if they believe you
  to be impostors.
* Stojanow Gate: setting off the alarm here should start random encounters and
  alert the tower guards, but it doesn't.
* Stojanow Gate: confronting the tower guards after defeating the bugbear
  patrol has you fight both sets of tower guards, even if you have already
  defeated one set.
* Stojanow Gate: fleeing the bugbear patrol after fighting the tower guards
  crashes the game.
* Valjevo Castle Hedge Maze: at the false Tyranthraxus, the logic for
  "P"arleying with an "A"busive attitude is backwards.
* Valjevo Castle Inner Tower: the game script does not properly flag
  Tyranthraxus as defeated.

SECTION 13.3 - Adaptational Bugs
--------------------------------

This only considers adaptational errors that would be relatively easy to fix.
The game does correctly enforce some rather obscure rules; even so, I'm
excluding many difficult to enforce rules from the below.

* Minimum statistics required for a class are not fully enforced.  (For
  example, for classes available in any Gold Box game, Charisma 6 should be
  required.)
* No aging penalties are applied to statistics (even though some code exists
  for this).
* No death from old age (even though some code exists for this).
* No Intelligence roll is required to learn a magic-user spell.
* No clerical spell failure for Wisdom below 13.
* Dexterity 10 Pick Pocket adjustment should be -10%, not -19%.
* Dexterity 16 Open Locks adjustment should be +5%, not -5%.
* In cases where you lose a Dexterity bonus to Armor Class, you lose even
  penalties.
* Clerics should not be allowed to be True Neutral.
* Thieves are not required to refrain from using shields to act as a thief.
* Being blind should cause you to treat all creatures as invisible.
* Hold Person should require a saving throw vs. paralyzation, not vs. spell.
* Spiritual Hammer always has a flat +1 magical bonus; it should strike as a
  magical hammer for vulnerability purposes, but otherwise with no bonus.
* Animate Dead is far more powerful than it is supposed to be; it animates
  the dead with full hit points at significant range.
* Cause Disease does not last indefinitely, but instead 1d6 * 10 minutes, which
  should instead be the time of onset.
* Raise Dead: (1) has no chance of failure (fixed in Secret of the Silver
  Blades); (2) should not work on elves.
* Enlarge should grant slightly lower Strength at level 1 and 2.
* Reduce should do more than just dispel Enlarge.
* Shield provides a fixed, not varied, AC.
* Ray of Enfeeblement reduces damage by 25%; it does not reduce damage by an
  additional 2%/level.
* Haste and Slow do not affect initiative as they should.
* Protection from Normal Missiles should also mitigate damage from magical
  thrown and launched weaponry.
* A bec de corbin should either bash or pierce, not slash.
* A military fork, ranseur, or trident should pierce, not slash.
* A Cloak of Displacement should also grant a saving throw bonus.  (Any found
  as random treasure actually do, but non-random ones do not.)
* The Cloak of Elvenkind should check for whether its wearer is outside.
* Padded armor allows base movement 12, not 9 as in tabletop.
* Magical armor never provides a saving throw bonus.
* A Ring of Protection rarely adds its bonus to saving throws.
* A Wand of Paralyzation should permit a save vs. rod/staff/wand, not
  paralyzation.
* A monster equipping an item should receive any effects it grants.
* A monster equipping armor should have its movement reduced appropriately.
* LARGE SCORPION (#18) and HUGE SCORPION (#39) should not be snakes.
* TROLL (#31) should not be able to return from the dead after taking fire or
  acid damage.  (Fixed in Curse of the Azure Bonds; it only works here if *all*
  damage is fire/acid.)
* DISPLACER BEAST (#68) should be displaced.
* DISPLACER BEAST (#68) should not be lawful good.
* DRIDER (#69) does not penalize the saving throw vs. paralyzation.
* EFREETI (#70 and #124) does not know how to cast Enlarge or Invisibility.
* The MUMMY (#114) fear aura saving throw is supposed to consider the ratio of
  opponents to mummies, not just the number of opponents.

SECTION 13.4 - Strange Programming Decisions
--------------------------------------------

Points are only put here if I know it's on SSI's end.  Many problems are the
compiler's fault, others of the Pascal programming language; this is running
long enough as it is without getting into those.

* Strings should not usually be passed by value, if your hardware is limited.
  The syntactic distinction is (using C++ for comparison):
                   pass by value     pass by reference
  C++              (string S)        (string &S)     // with #include <string>
  Turbo Pascal     (S: string)       (var S: string)
* Effect and Creature probably shouldn't be stored as linked lists in
  dynamically allocated heap memory; that has serious overhead costs.
* Much the same applies to allocating combat information separately.
* Some variables that are only briefly needed are defined as global variables.
  Several are File variables; those take significant space.  (Many global
  variables are defensible in general; you'd otherwise need a lot more
  indirection or many more stack variables, which is costly on older
  architecture.)
* Some functions are identical, or very nearly so (e.g. there are multiple
  functions to input a number).
* The constantly used function that gets keystrokes does a String comparison to
  check for STING on every single keypress, which could be done once ever.
* I think it's perfectly defensible to roll your own graphics routines; you had
  to anyway if you were ever commercially releasing a game using Turbo Pascal.
  But after SSI makes the sensible decision to commit to a graphics mode during
  initialization, the game then loads a bunch of machine code and data for the
  other two modes anyway that will never get used.  You get better performance
  with a single dedicated graphics driver, taking up less memory.
* SSI apparently planned on using multiple EGA pages, but only ended up using
  one.  That's fine, there were clearly many things planned but never put in
  ... but the resulting code performs many EGA writes to that one page twice
  for no reason.  That's especially awkward since EGA already requires four
  writes to display one row of pixels.  This is likely the biggest contributor
  to text being printed so slowly compared to later Gold Box games.
* Why are some bytes in the GBVM 16-bit and others 8-bit?  That might be partly
  defensible, except it matters both what you are accessing and how (whether
  you are reading or writing, what you are writing, etc.)  That inconsistency
  I'd expect to cause trouble both fixing bugs and adapting the engine to later
  games.

SECTION 14.1 - Journal Entries
------------------------------

Here is a list of Journal Entries actually referenced by the game, with where
to find them (along with what you need to do to get it if it is not automatic).
Note that information in these entries is not guaranteed to be true.

 3 - Sokal Keep 16 (speak correctly to the keep's spectral defenders)
 7 - Mendor's Library 2 (search Philosophy section)
 8 - Mendor's Library 4 (search History section)
10 - Mendor's Library 18 (accept kobolds' surrender)
11 - Cadorna Textile House 23

16 - Kobold Caves 14
19 - Mendor's Library 2 (search Philosophy section)
20 - Kobold Caves 18 (give water to crippled kobold)
21 - Mendor's Library 4 (search History section)
23 - Kovel Mansion 10 (open cabinet A)
25 - Temple of Bane (defeat Mace)
26 - Yarash's Pyramid Level 1 13 (search remains)
27 - Yarash's Pyramid Level 3 27 (search Yarash's desk)
28 - Kobold Caves 13
29 - Kovel Mansion 20
30 - Valjevo Castle Hedge Maze 1 (threaten Al-Hyam Dazid until he offers notes)

35 - Yarash's Pyramid Level 3 24 (befriend and free enslaved lizardmen)
37 - Mendor's Library 4 (search History section)

41 - Kovel Mansion 19
